key_insight: The paper shows that convex-concave saddle functions can be approximated by a structured separable form that preserves convexity in x and concavity in y by design.
engineering_relevance: This enables reliable min-max models for optimization, optimal transport, robust learning, and control, where geometry-preserving approximations improve tractability and certificate quality.
method: Prove a one-dimensional approximation theorem under a mixed Monge-type convexity condition.
Build saddle networks using convexity-preserving subnetworks plus simple output transforms to enforce sign and concavity.
Use a separable architecture that keeps the convex-concave structure intact throughout training and inference.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical benchmarks in dimensions 1 and 5, with high accuracy on smooth, nonsmooth, and high-rank convex-concave test functions.
watch_item: Performance may depend on satisfying the mixed Monge-type condition and on carefully implementing the sign/concavity constraints.
paper_title: Saddle Networks: Structure-Preserving Architectures for Convex-Concave Functions
